perturbation.original_text: Each Party irrevocably consents to the exclusive jurisdiction, forum and venue of any state or federal court sitting in New York City in the State of New York over any and all claims, disputes, controversies or disagreements between the Parties or any of their respective Affiliates, successors and assigns under or related to this Agreement or any of the transactions contemplated hereby, including their execution, performance or enforcement, whether in contract, tort or otherwise. Each of the Parties hereby agrees that it shall not assert and shall hereby waive any claim or right or defense that it is not subject to the jurisdiction of such courts, that the venue is improper, that the forum is inconvenient or any similar objection, claim or argument.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party consents to the jurisdiction, forum and venue of a court selected by Honeywell over any claims, disputes, controversies or disagreements between the Parties under or related to this Agreement. Each of the Parties agrees that it shall not assert any defense that it is not subject to the jurisdiction of such courts.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specification of New York City courts and giving Honeywell the power to select the forum, the contract becomes potentially non-compliant with laws ensuring fair venue selection and could unduly burden SpinCo if the chosen venue is unreasonably distant or inaccessible, violating principles of procedural fairness.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Forum selection clauses must be reasonable and not unduly burdensome.
perturbation.law_explanation: This modification contradicts 28 U.S. Code § 1404, which addresses change of venue for the convenience of parties and witnesses, in the interest of justice.  The changed text allows Honeywell to choose a venue that may be highly inconvenient or unjust for SpinCo, undermining the principles of fairness and convenience that underpin venue selection under federal law. The official link shows the law itself, while the secondary source details related cases.

(b) Upon motion, consent or stipulation of all parties, any action, suit or proceeding of a civil nature or any motion or hearing thereof, may be transferred, in t
perturbation.justification: The modified text contradicts 28 U.S. Code § 1404, which addresses change of venue for the convenience of parties and witnesses and in the interest of justice. The original text specified venue in New York City, while the changed text allows Honeywell to choose the venue, potentially inconvenient or unjust for the other party (SpinCo). This undermines the principles of fairness that underpin venue selection under federal law. As stated in the snippet, 'For the convenience of parties and witnesses, in the interest of justice, a district court may transfer any civil action to any other district or division where it might have been brought or to any district or division to which all parties have consented.'

perturbation.original_text: EACH PARTY HEREBY WAIVES ITS RIGHTS TO A JURY TRIAL OF ANY CLAIM OR CAUSE OF ACTION BASED UPON, ARISING OUT OF OR RELATING TO THIS AGREEMENT OR THE TRANSACTIONS CONTEMPLATED HEREBY, INCLUDING THEIR EXECUTION, PERFORMANCE OR ENFORCEMENT, WHETHER IN CONTRACT, TORT OR OTHERWISE. THE SCOPE OF THIS WAIVER IS INTENDED TO BE ALL-ENCOMPASSING OF ANY AND ALL DISPUTES THAT MAY BE FILED IN ANY COURT AND THAT RELATE TO THE SUBJECT MATTER OF THIS TRANSACTION, INCLUDING CONTRACT CLAIMS, TORT CLAIMS (INCLUDING NEGLIGENCE), BREACH OF DUTY CLAIMS AND ALL OTHER COMMON LAW AND STATUTORY CLAIMS. THIS SECTION HAS BEEN FULLY DISCUSSED BY EACH OF THE PARTIES AND THESE PROVISIONS WILL NOT BE SUBJECT TO ANY EXCEPTIONS.
perturbation.changed_text: SpinCo waives its rights to a jury trial of any claim or cause of action based upon, arising out of or relating to this Agreement. This provision will not be subject to any exceptions.
perturbation.explanation: Altering the jury trial waiver to apply solely to SpinCo introduces an imbalance of power, potentially violating standards of good faith and fair dealing. Such a one-sided waiver can be deemed unconscionable, especially if it disadvantages SpinCo in potential litigation.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Waivers of jury trial must be knowing, voluntary, and not unconscionable.
perturbation.law_explanation: The modified text undermines the requirements for a valid jury trial waiver.  N.Y. Const. Art. I, § 2, guarantees the right to a jury trial, and any waiver must be knowing and voluntary. By making the waiver only applicable to SpinCo, the agreement creates a situation where it could be argued that the waiver was not entered into freely and knowingly, and may be deemed unconscionable. The one-sided nature of the waiver raises concerns about fairness and equal bargaining power. The first link explains the right to jury trial in New York while the second offers a deeper discussion on the topic.

Article I, New York Constitution From Ballotpedia Jump to: navigation , search New York Constitution Preamble Articles I • II • III • IV • V • VI • VII • VIII • IX • X • XI • XII • XIII • XIV • XV • XVI • XVII • XVIII • XIX • XX Contents 1 Section 1 1.1 Amendments 2 Section 2 2.1 Amendments 3 Section 3 3.1 Amendments 4 Section 4 4.1 Amendments 5 Section 5 6 Section 6 6.1 Amendments 7 Section 7 7.1 Amendments 8 Section 8 8.1 Amendments 9 Section 9 9.1 Amendments 10 Section 10 11 Section 11 11.1 Amendments 12 Section 12 12.1 Amendments 13 Section 13 14 Section 14 14.1 Amendments 15 Section 15 16 Section 16 16.1 Amendments 17 Section 17 17.1 Amendments 18 Section 18 18.1 Amendments 19 Section 19 19.1 Amendments 20 See also 21 External links 22 Additional reading 23 Footnotes Article I of the New York Constitution is entitled Bill of Rights and consists of 18 sections, three of which have been repealed. Section 1 Text of Section 1: Rights, Privileges and Franchise Secured; Power of Legislature to Dispense with Primary Elections in Certain Cases No member of this state shall be disfranchised, or deprived of any of the rights or privileges secured to any citizen thereof, unless by the law of the land, or the judgment of his or her peers, except that the legislature may provide that there shall be no primary election held to nominate candidates for public office or to elect persons to party positions for any political party or parties in any unit of representation of the state from which such candidates or persons are nominated or elected whenever there is no contest or contests for such nominations or election as may be prescribed by general law. [1] Amendments Amended by vote of the people on November 3, 1959. Amended by vote of the people on November 6, 2001. Section 2 Text of Section 2: Trial by Jury; How Waived Trial by jury in all cases in which it has heretofore been guaranteed by constitutional provision shall remain inviolate forever; but a jury trial may be waived by the parties in all civil cases in the manner to be prescribed by law. The legislature may provide, however, by law, that a verdict may be rendered by not less than five-sixths of the jury in any civil case. A jury trial may be waived by the defendant in all cri
perturbation.justification: The modified text creates a contradiction by making a jury trial waiver applicable only to SpinCo, which undermines the principle that such waivers must be knowing and voluntary. N.Y. Const. Art. I, § 2, as shown in 'Scraped Law Snippet 2,' guarantees the right to a jury trial, and a waiver should not be structured in a way that suggests unequal bargaining power or lack of free and knowing consent. The original contract had both parties waiving their rights which makes the change contradictory.
